1‚Üí"""Authentication module for Maimai login.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíIn CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
     4‚ÜíCLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
     5‚Üíif the user is not logged in.
     6‚Üí"""
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üíimport asyncio
     9‚Üífrom typing import Optional
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üífrom patchright.async_api import Page
    12‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    13‚Üífrom rich.console import Console
    14‚Üífrom rich.panel import Panel
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üífrom src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17‚Üífrom src.core.session import get_session_manager
    18‚Üífrom src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    19‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üíconsole = Console()
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üíasync def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    25‚Üí    """
    26‚Üí    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in.
    29‚Üí    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí    Args:
    32‚Üí        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí    Returns:
    35‚Üí        True if logged in
    36‚Üí    """
    37‚Üí    if browser is None:
    38‚Üí        browser = get_browser_manager()
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    41‚Üí    page = await browser.start()
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí    # Check if already logged in
    44‚Üí    if await check_login_status(page):
    45‚Üí        console.print("[green]Already logged in![/green]")
    46‚Üí        # Save session as backup
    47‚Üí        await browser.save_session()
    48‚Üí        return True
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí    # Not logged in ‚Äî guide the user
    51‚Üí    console.print(Panel(
    52‚Üí        "[bold]Not Logged In[/bold]\n\n"
    53‚Üí        "Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser:\n"
    54‚Üí        "1. Go to https://maimai.cn/login in Chrome\n"
    55‚Üí        "2. Scan QR code with Maimai app, OR\n"
    56‚Üí        "3. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
    57‚Üí        "[dim]Then run this command again to verify.[/dim]",
    58‚Üí        title="Maimai Login",
    59‚Üí        border_style="cyan"
    60‚Üí    ))
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí    # Poll for login (user may log in while we wait)
    63‚Üí    max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
    64‚Üí    poll_interval = 3
    65‚Üí    elapsed = 0
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí    console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
    70‚Üí        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
    71‚Üí        elapsed += poll_interval
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí        # Check for captcha
    74‚Üí        await check_captcha(page)
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí        if await check_login_status(page):
    77‚Üí            console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
    78‚Üí            await browser.save_session()
    79‚Üí            console.print("[green]Session saved as backup[/green]")
    80‚Üí            return True
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí    console.print("[red]Login timeout. Please log in manually and try again.[/red]")
    83‚Üí    return False
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíasync def check_login_status(page: Page) -> bool:
    87‚Üí    """
    88‚Üí    Check if user is currently logged in by navigating to a protected page
    89‚Üí    and observing whether Maimai redirects to the login page.
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí    API probes can give false positives (server returns 200 but session is
    92‚Üí    actually stale). The redirect-based approach is reliable because Maimai's
    93‚Üí    server-side auth check only fires on real page navigations, and it will
    94‚Üí    redirect to the login page if the session is invalid.
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí    Args:
    97‚Üí        page: Playwright page instance
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üí    Returns:
   100‚Üí        True if logged in (no redirect to login page)
   101‚Üí    """
   102‚Üí    try:
   103‚Üí        # Navigate to a protected page that requires authentication.
   104‚Üí        # The search center page reliably triggers server-side auth checks.
   105‚Üí        protected_url = "https://maimai.cn/web/search_center"
   106‚Üí        current_url = page.url
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí        # Only navigate if we're not already on a protected maimai page
   109‚Üí        if 'maimai.cn' not in current_url or 'login' in current_url:
   110‚Üí            await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   111‚Üí        else:
   112‚Üí            # We're on maimai but need to trigger an auth check.
   113‚Üí            # Navigate to the protected page to force the server-side check.
   114‚Üí            await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí        # Wait for any redirects to settle (server-side auth redirect happens
   117‚Üí        # after initial page load and JS execution)
   118‚Üí        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí        # Check 1: URL-based detection - did we get redirected to login?
   121‚Üí        final_url = page.url
   122‚Üí        if 'login' in final_url.lower():
   123‚Üí            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=redirected_to_login (url={final_url})")
   124‚Üí            return False
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí        # Check 2: Page content detection - look for login form elements
   127‚Üí        # even if URL didn't change (some login overlays don't change the URL)
   128‚Üí        is_login_page = await page.evaluate("""
   129‚Üí            () => {
   130‚Üí                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
   131‚Üí                const loginIndicators = [
   132‚Üí                    'Ê¨¢Ëøé‰ΩøÁî®ËÑâËÑâ',
   133‚Üí                    'ÁôªÂΩï/Ê≥®ÂÜå',
   134‚Üí                    'ËØ∑ËæìÂÖ•ÊâãÊú∫Âè∑',
   135‚Üí                    'Ëé∑ÂèñÈ™åËØÅÁÝÅ',
   136‚Üí                    'Êâ´ÁÝÅÁôªÂΩï',
   137‚Üí                    'ÊâãÊú∫Âè∑ÁôªÂΩï'
   138‚Üí                ];
   139‚Üí                for (const indicator of loginIndicators) {
   140‚Üí                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
   141‚Üí                        return { is_login: true, matched: indicator };
   142‚Üí                    }
   143‚Üí                }
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí                // Also check for login form elements in DOM
   146‚Üí                const loginForm = document.querySelector(
   147‚Üí                    'input[type="tel"], input[placeholder*="ÊâãÊú∫"], ' +
   148‚Üí                    '[class*="qrcode"], [class*="scan-login"]'
   149‚Üí                );
   150‚Üí                if (loginForm) {
   151‚Üí                    return { is_login: true, matched: 'login_form_element' };
   152‚Üí                }
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí                return { is_login: false, matched: null };
   155‚Üí            }
   156‚Üí        """)
   157‚Üí
   158‚Üí        if is_login_page.get('is_login', False):
   159‚Üí            detail = is_login_page.get('matched', 'unknown')
   160‚Üí            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=login_page_detected ({detail})")
   161‚Üí            return False
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí        logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=True, detail=protected_page_accessible (url={final_url})")
   164‚Üí        return True
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí    except Exception as e:
   167‚Üí        logger.error(f"Error checking login status: {e}")
   168‚Üí        return False
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üíasync def ensure_logged_in(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
   172‚Üí    """
   173‚Üí    Ensure user is logged in, prompting for login if needed.
   174‚Üí
   175‚Üí    Args:
   176‚Üí        browser: Browser manager instance
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí    Returns:
   179‚Üí        True if logged in (existing or new login)
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí    Raises:
   182‚Üí        SessionExpiredError: If login fails
   183‚Üí    """
   184‚Üí    if browser is None:
   185‚Üí        browser = get_browser_manager()
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí    # Connect to Chrome via CDP if not already connected
   188‚Üí    if not browser.is_running:
   189‚Üí        await browser.start()
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí    # Check login status
   192‚Üí    if browser.page:
   193‚Üí        if await check_login_status(browser.page):
   194‚Üí            return True
   195‚Üí
   196‚Üí    # Not logged in ‚Äî guide user
   197‚Üí    console.print("[yellow]Not logged in. Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser.[/yellow]")
   198‚Üí
   199‚Üí    if await login(browser):
   200‚Üí        return True
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí    raise SessionExpiredError("Not logged in to Maimai. Please log in manually in Chrome and try again.")
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üíasync def logout(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> None:
   206‚Üí    """
   207‚Üí    Log out and clear saved session backup.
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí    Args:
   210‚Üí        browser: Browser manager instance
   211‚Üí    """
   212‚Üí    if browser is None:
   213‚Üí        browser = get_browser_manager()
   214‚Üí
   215‚Üí    session_manager = get_session_manager()
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí    # Clear saved session backup
   218‚Üí    await session_manager.clear()
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí    # Disconnect from Chrome (don't close it)
   221‚Üí    await browser.close()
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí    console.print("[green]Session backup cleared. To fully log out, clear cookies in Chrome.[/green]")
   224‚Üí

1‚Üí#!/usr/bin/env python
     2‚Üí"""Main CLI entry point for Maimai Assistant."""
     3‚Üí
     4‚Üíimport sys
     5‚Üíimport asyncio
     6‚Üífrom pathlib import Path
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üíimport click
     9‚Üífrom rich.console import Console
    10‚Üífrom rich.table import Table
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí# Add src to path
    13‚Üísys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent))
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üífrom src.utils.logger import setup_logger
    16‚Üífrom src.utils.config import get_config
    17‚Üífrom src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter
    18‚Üífrom src.utils.delays import reset_session as reset_delay_session
    19‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import MaimaiAssistantError
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üíconsole = Console()
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üídef run_async(coro):
    25‚Üí    """Run async function in sync context."""
    26‚Üí    return asyncio.run(coro)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üídef _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx):
    30‚Üí    """Apply CDP endpoint override from CLI --cdp flag to browser manager."""
    31‚Üí    cdp_endpoint = ctx.obj.get('cdp_endpoint') if ctx.obj else None
    32‚Üí    if cdp_endpoint:
    33‚Üí        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    34‚Üí        get_browser_manager().set_cdp_endpoint(cdp_endpoint)
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí@click.group()
    38‚Üí@click.version_option(version='0.1.0')
    39‚Üí@click.option('--cdp', 'cdp_endpoint', default=None, envvar='MAIMAI_CDP_ENDPOINT',
    40‚Üí              help='CDP endpoint URL (default: http://localhost:9222)')
    41‚Üí@click.pass_context
    42‚Üídef cli(ctx, cdp_endpoint):
    43‚Üí    """
    44‚Üí    Maimai Semi-Automated Recruitment Assistant
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí    Connects to your Chrome browser via CDP. Start Chrome with:
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí    Then log in to Maimai manually before running commands.
    51‚Üí    """
    52‚Üí    try:
    53‚Üí        # Initialize configuration and logging
    54‚Üí        setup_logger()
    55‚Üí        ctx.ensure_object(dict)
    56‚Üí        ctx.obj['cdp_endpoint'] = cdp_endpoint
    57‚Üí    except Exception as e:
    58‚Üí        console.print(f"[red]Failed to initialize application: {e}[/red]")
    59‚Üí        sys.exit(1)
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí@cli.command()
    63‚Üí@click.pass_context
    64‚Üídef login(ctx):
    65‚Üí    """
    66‚Üí    Verify login status via CDP connection.
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí    Connects to your Chrome browser and checks if you're logged in to Maimai.
    69‚Üí    If not, waits for you to log in manually in the browser.
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí    First, start Chrome with remote debugging:
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí    Then log in to Maimai in that Chrome window.
    76‚Üí    """
    77‚Üí    from src.modules.auth import login as do_login
    78‚Üí    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí    async def _login():
    83‚Üí        browser = get_browser_manager()
    84‚Üí        try:
    85‚Üí            success = await do_login(browser)
    86‚Üí            if success:
    87‚Üí                console.print("[green]Login verified![/green]")
    88‚Üí            else:
    89‚Üí                console.print("[red]Login failed or timed out.[/red]")
    90‚Üí                sys.exit(1)
    91‚Üí        finally:
    92‚Üí            await browser.close()
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí    run_async(_login())
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí@cli.command()
    98‚Üí@click.option('--keywords', '-k', required=True, multiple=True, help='Search keywords (can specify multiple times)')
    99‚Üí@click.option('--company', '-c', multiple=True, help='Filter by company name (can specify multiple)')
   100‚Üí@click.option('--location', '-l', help='Filter by location')
   101‚Üí@click.option('--max-pages', '-p', type=int, default=1, help='Maximum pages to scrape per keyword')
   102‚Üí@click.option('--mode', '-m', type=click.Choice(['search_center', 'enterprise']), default='search_center',
   103‚Üí              help='Search mode: search_center (default) or enterprise')
   104‚Üí@click.option('--export', '-e', help='Export results to file (Excel/CSV)')
   105‚Üí@click.pass_context
   106‚Üídef search(ctx, keywords, company, location, max_pages, mode, export):
   107‚Üí    """
   108‚Üí    Search for candidates with specified criteria.
   109‚Üí

   118‚Üí    """

1‚Üí"""Configuration loader and manager."""
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport json
     4‚Üífrom pathlib import Path
     5‚Üífrom typing import Any, Dict
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üífrom loguru import logger
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import ConfigurationError
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üíclass Config:
    13‚Üí    """Configuration manager for Maimai Assistant."""
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí    def __init__(self, config_dir: str = "config"):
    16‚Üí        self.config_dir = Path(config_dir)
    17‚Üí        self._settings: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    18‚Üí        self._selectors: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    19‚Üí        self._message_templates: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí        self._load_all()
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí    def _load_json(self, filename: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    24‚Üí        """Load JSON configuration file."""
    25‚Üí        filepath = self.config_dir / filename
    26‚Üí        try:
    27‚Üí            with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    28‚Üí                return json.load(f)
    29‚Üí        except FileNotFoundError:
    30‚Üí            raise ConfigurationError(f"Configuration file not found: {filepath}")
    31‚Üí        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    32‚Üí            raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid JSON in {filepath}: {e}")
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí    def _load_all(self):
    35‚Üí        """Load all configuration files."""
    36‚Üí        logger.info("Loading configuration files...")
    37‚Üí        self._settings = self._load_json("settings.json")
    38‚Üí        self._selectors = self._load_json("selectors.json")
    39‚Üí        self._message_templates = self._load_json("message_templates.json")
    40‚Üí        logger.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    43‚Üí        """Get setting by dot-notation key (e.g., 'browser.headless')."""
    44‚Üí        keys = key.split('.')
    45‚Üí        value = self._settings
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí        for k in keys:
    48‚Üí            if isinstance(value, dict):
    49‚Üí                value = value.get(k)
    50‚Üí                if value is None:
    51‚Üí                    return default
    52‚Üí            else:
    53‚Üí                return default
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí        return value
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí    def get_selector(self, selector_path: str) -> str:
    58‚Üí        """Get selector by dot-notation path (e.g., 'search.keyword_input')."""
    59‚Üí        keys = selector_path.split('.')
    60‚Üí        value = self._selectors.get('selectors', {})
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí        for k in keys:
    63‚Üí            if isinstance(value, dict):
    64‚Üí                value = value.get(k)
    65‚Üí                if value is None:
    66‚Üí                    raise ConfigurationError(f"Selector not found: {selector_path}")
    67‚Üí            else:
    68‚Üí                raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid selector path: {selector_path}")
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí        return value
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí    def get_template(self, template_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    73‚Üí        """Get message template by name."""
    74‚Üí        template = self._message_templates.get(template_name)
    75‚Üí        if template is None:
    76‚Üí            raise ConfigurationError(f"Message template not found: {template_name}")
    77‚Üí        return template
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí    def list_templates(self) -> list:
    80‚Üí        """List all available message template names."""
    81‚Üí        return list(self._message_templates.keys())
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí# Global configuration instance
    85‚Üí_config_instance = None
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üídef get_config() -> Config:
    89‚Üí    """Get global configuration instance."""
    90‚Üí    global _config_instance
    91‚Üí    if _config_instance is None:
    92‚Üí        _config_instance = Config()
    93‚Üí    return _config_instance
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Input validation utilities."""
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport re
     4‚Üífrom typing import Optional, Dict, Any
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import ValidationError
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üídef validate_maimai_id(maimai_id: str) -> str:
    10‚Üí    """
    11‚Üí    Validate Maimai user ID format.
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí    Args:
    14‚Üí        maimai_id: Maimai user ID to validate
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí    Returns:
    17‚Üí        Validated and cleaned ID
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí    Raises:
    20‚Üí        ValidationError: If ID format is invalid
    21‚Üí    """
    22‚Üí    if not maimai_id:
    23‚Üí        raise ValidationError("Maimai ID cannot be empty")
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí    # Remove whitespace
    26‚Üí    maimai_id = str(maimai_id).strip()
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí    # Check if it's alphanumeric
    29‚Üí    if not re.match(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$', maimai_id):
    30‚Üí        raise ValidationError(f"Invalid Maimai ID format: {maimai_id}")
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí    return maimai_id
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üídef validate_keywords(keywords: str) -> str:
    36‚Üí    """
    37‚Üí    Validate search keywords.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí    Args:
    40‚Üí        keywords: Search keywords
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí    Returns:
    43‚Üí        Validated keywords
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí    Raises:
    46‚Üí        ValidationError: If keywords are invalid
    47‚Üí    """
    48‚Üí    if not keywords or not keywords.strip():
    49‚Üí        raise ValidationError("Search keywords cannot be empty")
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí    keywords = keywords.strip()
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí    # Check length
    54‚Üí    if len(keywords) < 2:
    55‚Üí        raise ValidationError("Search keywords must be at least 2 characters")
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí    if len(keywords) > 200:
    58‚Üí        raise ValidationError("Search keywords cannot exceed 200 characters")
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí    return keywords
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üídef validate_template_variables(template: Dict[str, Any], variables: Dict[str, str]) -> None:
    64‚Üí    """
    65‚Üí    Validate that all required template variables are provided.
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí    Args:
    68‚Üí        template: Template dictionary with 'variables' key
    69‚Üí        variables: Provided variables dictionary
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí    Raises:
    72‚Üí        ValidationError: If required variables are missing
    73‚Üí    """
    74‚Üí    required_vars = template.get('variables', [])
    75‚Üí    provided_vars = set(variables.keys())
    76‚Üí    required_set = set(required_vars)
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí    missing_vars = required_set - provided_vars
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí    if missing_vars:
    81‚Üí        raise ValidationError(
    82‚Üí            f"Missing required template variables: {', '.join(missing_vars)}"
    83‚Üí        )
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üídef validate_file_path(
    87‚Üí    file_path: str,
    88‚Üí    extension: Optional[str] = None,
    89‚Üí    allowed_dir: Optional[str] = None,
    90‚Üí) -> str:
    91‚Üí    """
    92‚Üí    Validate file path format and prevent path traversal.
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí    Resolves the path to an absolute form and ensures it falls within
    95‚Üí    an allowed directory (defaults to current working directory).
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí    Args:
    98‚Üí        file_path: File path to validate
    99‚Üí        extension: Expected file extension (e.g., '.xlsx', '.csv')
   100‚Üí        allowed_dir: Directory the resolved path must reside in.
   101‚Üí                     Defaults to current working directory.
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí    Returns:
   104‚Üí        Validated, resolved file path
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí    Raises:
   107‚Üí        ValidationError: If path is invalid or escapes allowed directory
   108‚Üí    """
   109‚Üí    from pathlib import Path
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí    if not file_path or not file_path.strip():
   112‚Üí        raise ValidationError("File path cannot be empty")
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí    file_path = file_path.strip()
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí    # Check for shell metacharacters that could cause injection
   117‚Üí    dangerous_chars = ['$', '`', '|', ';', '&']
   118‚Üí    if any(char in file_path for char in dangerous_chars):
   119‚Üí        raise ValidationError(f"File path contains dangerous characters: {file_path}")
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üí    # Resolve to absolute path and check traversal
   122‚Üí    resolved = Path(file_path).resolve()
   123‚Üí    if allowed_dir is None:
   124‚Üí        allowed = Path.cwd().resolve()
   125‚Üí    else:
   126‚Üí        allowed = Path(allowed_dir).resolve()
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üí    if not str(resolved).startswith(str(allowed) + '/') and resolved != allowed:
   129‚Üí        raise ValidationError(
   130‚Üí            f"File path must be within {allowed}: {file_path}"
   131‚Üí        )
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí    # Check extension if specified
   134‚Üí    if extension:
   135‚Üí        if not extension.startswith('.'):
   136‚Üí            extension = f'.{extension}'
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí        if not resolved.name.lower().endswith(extension):
   139‚Üí            raise ValidationError(
   140‚Üí                f"File path must have {extension} extension: {file_path}"
   141‚Üí            )
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí    return str(resolved)
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí
   146‚Üí

    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí    # Relationships
    46‚Üí    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí    def __repr__(self):
    49‚Üí        return f"<Candidate(maimai_id='{self.maimai_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üíclass Message(Base):
    53‚Üí    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí    __tablename__ = 'messages'
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)

    59‚Üí    template_name = Column(String(100))
    60‚Üí    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    61‚Üí    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    62‚Üí    retry_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    63‚Üí    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    64‚Üí    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí    # Relationships
    67‚Üí    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí    def __repr__(self):
    70‚Üí        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_maimai_id}', status='{self.status}')>"
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üíclass SearchHistory(Base):

    75‚Üí

    95‚Üí    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details

    97‚Üí    # Composite index for efficient rate limiting queries

1‚Üí"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport asyncio
     4‚Üífrom datetime import datetime, timedelta
     5‚Üífrom typing import Tuple, Optional
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üífrom loguru import logger
     8‚Üífrom sqlalchemy import func
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üífrom src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
    11‚Üífrom src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    12‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üíclass RateLimiter:
    16‚Üí    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    19‚Üí    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    20‚Üí    """
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
    23‚Üí        """Initialize rate limiter with configuration.
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí        Args:
    26‚Üí            config: Config instance (uses global singleton if None)
    27‚Üí            db_manager: DatabaseManager instance (uses global singleton if None)
    28‚Üí        """
    29‚Üí        self.config = config or get_config()
    30‚Üí        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí        # Adaptive throttle state
    33‚Üí        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0  # 1.0 = normal, <1.0 = stricter
    34‚Üí        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
    35‚Üí        self._error_timestamps: list = []
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
    38‚Üí        """Report a captcha occurrence to trigger adaptive throttling."""
    39‚Üí        now = datetime.utcnow()
    40‚Üí        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
    41‚Üí        # Keep only last 30 minutes of captcha events
    42‚Üí        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    43‚Üí        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    44‚Üí        self._update_throttle_factor()
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí    def report_error(self) -> None:
    47‚Üí        """Report a request error/failure to trigger adaptive throttling."""
    48‚Üí        now = datetime.utcnow()
    49‚Üí        self._error_timestamps.append(now)
    50‚Üí        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    51‚Üí        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    52‚Üí        self._update_throttle_factor()
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
    55‚Üí        """Recalculate throttle factor based on recent captcha/error frequency.
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí        Throttle factor ranges from 1.0 (normal) to 0.3 (heavily throttled).
    58‚Üí        - 1 captcha in 30min: reduce to 0.7x (30% fewer operations allowed)
    59‚Üí        - 2 captchas: reduce to 0.5x
    60‚Üí        - 3+ captchas: reduce to 0.3x
    61‚Üí        - Errors contribute at half the weight of captchas
    62‚Üí        """
    63‚Üí        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
    64‚Üí        error_count = len(self._error_timestamps)
    65‚Üí        combined = captcha_count + error_count * 0.5
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí        if combined >= 3:
    68‚Üí            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
    69‚Üí        elif combined >= 2:
    70‚Üí            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
    71‚Üí        elif combined >= 1:
    72‚Üí            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
    73‚Üí        else:
    74‚Üí            self._throttle_factor = 1.0
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    77‚Üí            logger.warning(
    78‚Üí                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
    79‚Üí                f"(captchas={captcha_count}, errors={error_count} in last 30min)"
    80‚Üí            )
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
    83‚Üí        """Prune expired timestamps and recalculate throttle factor."""
    84‚Üí        cutoff = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(minutes=30)
    85‚Üí        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    86‚Üí        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    87‚Üí        self._update_throttle_factor()
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí    @property
    90‚Üí    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
    91‚Üí        """Current adaptive throttle factor (1.0 = normal, <1.0 = throttled)."""
    92‚Üí        return self._throttle_factor
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    95‚Üí        """
    96‚Üí        Get rate limits for an action type, adjusted by adaptive throttle.
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí        Args:
    99‚Üí            action_type: Type of action ('search', 'view_profile', 'send_message')
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí        Returns:
   102‚Üí            Tuple of (per_hour_limit, per_day_limit, min_interval_seconds)
   103‚Üí        """
   104‚Üí        action_map = {
   105‚Üí            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
   106‚Üí            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
   107‚Üí            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
   108‚Üí        }
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí        if action_type not in action_map:
   111‚Üí            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
   116‚Üí        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
   117‚Üí        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 30)
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí        # Apply adaptive throttle: reduce limits and increase interval
   120‚Üí        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
   121‚Üí        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
   122‚Üí        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
   123‚Üí            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
   128‚Üí        """
   129‚Üí        Count actions of a type since a given time.
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí        Args:
   132‚Üí            action_type: Type of action
   133‚Üí            since: Count actions since this time
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí        Returns:
   136‚Üí            Number of actions
   137‚Üí        """
   138‚Üí        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   139‚Üí            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
   140‚Üí                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   141‚Üí                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
   142‚Üí            ).scalar()

150‚Üí        Args:
   151‚Üí            action_type: Type of action, or None for any action
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí        Returns:
   154‚Üí            Timestamp of last action, or None if no actions
   155‚Üí        """
   156‚Üí        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   157‚Üí            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí            if action_type:
   160‚Üí                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üí            last_time = query.scalar()
   163‚Üí            return last_time
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
   166‚Üí        """
   167‚Üí        Check if action is allowed under rate limits.
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí        Args:
   170‚Üí            action_type: Type of action to check
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí        Raises:
   173‚Üí            RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit is exceeded
   174‚Üí        """
   175‚Üí        self._refresh_throttle()
   176‚Üí        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí        now = datetime.utcnow()
   179‚Üí        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   180‚Üí        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí        # Check hourly limit
   183‚Üí        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   184‚Üí        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   185‚Üí            # Calculate wait time until oldest action expires
   186‚Üí            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   187‚Üí                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   188‚Üí                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   189‚Üí                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   190‚Üí                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   191‚Üí
   192‚Üí                if oldest_in_window:
   193‚Üí                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
   194‚Üí                    wait_seconds = int((wait_until - now).total_seconds())
   195‚Üí                else:
   196‚Üí                    wait_seconds = 3600
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   199‚Üí                action_type=action_type,
   200‚Üí                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   201‚Üí                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   202‚Üí                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   203‚Üí            )
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí        # Check daily limit
   206‚Üí        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   207‚Üí        if daily_count >= per_day:
   208‚Üí            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   209‚Üí                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   210‚Üí                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   211‚Üí                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   212‚Üí                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí                if oldest_in_window:
   215‚Üí                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
   216‚Üí                    wait_seconds = int((wait_until - now).total_seconds())
   217‚Üí                else:
   218‚Üí                    wait_seconds = 86400
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   221‚Üí                action_type=action_type,
   222‚Üí                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   223‚Üí                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   224‚Üí                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   225‚Üí            )
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üí        # Check minimum interval
   228‚Üí        last_action = self._get_last_action_time()
   229‚Üí        if last_action:
   230‚Üí            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   231‚Üí            if elapsed < min_interval:
   232‚Üí                wait_seconds = int(min_interval - elapsed)
   233‚Üí                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   234‚Üí                    action_type='any',
   235‚Üí                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   236‚Üí                    message=f"Minimum interval not met. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   237‚Üí                )
   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   242‚Üí        """
   243‚Üí        Log an action to the database.
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí        Args:
   246‚Üí            action_type: Type of action
   247‚Üí            details: Optional action details
   248‚Üí        """
   249‚Üí        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   250‚Üí            action_log = ActionLog(
   251‚Üí                action_type=action_type,
   252‚Üí                timestamp=datetime.utcnow(),
   253‚Üí                details=details or {}
   254‚Üí            )
   255‚Üí            session.add(action_log)
   256‚Üí
   257‚Üí        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   260‚Üí        """
   261‚Üí        Get current quota status for an action type.
   262‚Üí
   263‚Üí        Args:
   264‚Üí            action_type: Type of action
   265‚Üí
   266‚Üí        Returns:
   267‚Üí            Dictionary with quota information
   268‚Üí        """
   269‚Üí        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   270‚Üí
   271‚Üí        now = datetime.utcnow()
   272‚Üí        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   273‚Üí        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   274‚Üí
   275‚Üí        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   276‚Üí        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   277‚Üí
   278‚Üí        return {
   279‚Üí            'action_type': action_type,
   280‚Üí            'hourly': {
   281‚Üí                'used': hourly_used,
   282‚Üí                'limit': per_hour,
   283‚Üí                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   284‚Üí            },
   285‚Üí            'daily': {
   286‚Üí                'used': daily_used,
   287‚Üí                'limit': per_day,
   288‚Üí                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   289‚Üí            }
   290‚Üí        }
   291‚Üí
   292‚Üí
   293‚Üíasync def async_check_rate_limit(action_type: str) -> None:
   294‚Üí    """
   295‚Üí    Check rate limit, waiting through any violations until the action is allowed.
   296‚Üí
   297‚Üí    Blocks until the rate limit clears ‚Äî never raises RateLimitExceededError.
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí    Args:
   300‚Üí        action_type: Type of action to check
   301‚Üí    """
   302‚Üí    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   303‚Üí    while True:
   304‚Üí        try:
   305‚Üí            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   306‚Üí            return
   307‚Üí        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   308‚Üí            wait = e.wait_time + 1  # +1s buffer
   309‚Üí            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying...")
   310‚Üí            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   311‚Üí
   312‚Üí
   313‚Üí# Global rate limiter instance
   314‚Üí_rate_limiter = None
   315‚Üí
   316‚Üí
   317‚Üídef get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   318‚Üí    """Get global rate limiter instance."""
   319‚Üí    global _rate_limiter
   320‚Üí    if _rate_limiter is None:
   321‚Üí        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   322‚Üí    return _rate_limiter
   323‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Message module for sending messages to candidates.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíHandles message template rendering, interactive confirmation,
     4‚Üíand message sending with full audit trail.
     5‚Üí"""
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üíimport asyncio
     8‚Üífrom datetime import datetime
     9‚Üífrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üíimport questionary
    12‚Üífrom patchright.async_api import Page
    13‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    14‚Üífrom rich.console import Console
    15‚Üífrom rich.panel import Panel
    16‚Üífrom rich.text import Text
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üífrom src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    19‚Üífrom src.core.anti_detection import human_type, human_click
    20‚Üífrom src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    21‚Üífrom src.utils.config import get_config
    22‚Üífrom src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter, async_check_rate_limit
    23‚Üífrom src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    24‚Üífrom src.utils.retry import retry_navigation
    25‚Üífrom src.utils.validators import validate_maimai_id, validate_template_variables
    26‚Üífrom src.models.database import Candidate, Message, get_db_manager
    27‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import (
    28‚Üí    RateLimitExceededError,
    29‚Üí    ValidationError,
    30‚Üí)
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíconsole = Console()
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí# Maimai profile URL (fallback)
    35‚ÜíMAIMAI_PROFILE_URL = "https://maimai.cn/contact/detail/{maimai_id}"
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí# Maimai chat URL - navigate directly to chat page
    38‚ÜíMAIMAI_CHAT_URL = "https://maimai.cn/bizmember/uh_chat_pc?u2={user_id}&from=pc_web_search&fr=profile"
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üídef _extract_user_id(profile_url: str) -> Optional[str]:
    42‚Üí    """Extract dstu/user_id from a Maimai profile URL for direct chat navigation.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí    Only returns IDs that are known to work with the chat URL (u2= parameter).
    45‚Üí    Query-param IDs (dstu, u2, egid) are reliable for chat navigation.
    46‚Üí    Path-based IDs (/contact/detail/xxx) are NOT the same as chat user IDs
    47‚Üí    and should not be used for direct chat URL construction.
    48‚Üí    """
    49‚Üí    from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs
    50‚Üí    parsed = urlparse(profile_url)
    51‚Üí    params = parse_qs(parsed.query)
    52‚Üí    # Only use query parameter IDs ‚Äî these are confirmed to work with uh_chat_pc
    53‚Üí    for key in ('dstu', 'u2', 'egid'):
    54‚Üí        if key in params:
    55‚Üí            return params[key][0]
    56‚Üí    return None
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üíasync def send_message(
    60‚Üí    maimai_id: str,
    61‚Üí    template_name: str,
    62‚Üí    variables: Dict[str, str],
    63‚Üí    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
    64‚Üí    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
    65‚Üí    interactive: bool = False,

1‚Üí"""Random delay generators for human-like behavior simulation.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíUses log-normal distribution instead of uniform for more realistic timing.
     4‚ÜíIncludes fatigue simulation (delays increase over session duration) and
     5‚Üírandom "distraction" pauses to mimic real human behavior.
     6‚Üí"""
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üíimport math
     9‚Üíimport random
    10‚Üíimport asyncio
    11‚Üíimport time
    12‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    13‚Üífrom src.utils.config import get_config
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí# Session start time for fatigue calculation
    17‚Üí_session_start: float = time.monotonic()
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí# Action counter for distraction triggers
    20‚Üí_action_count: int = 0
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üídef _reset_session():
    24‚Üí    """Reset session tracking. Call at the start of each CLI command."""
    25‚Üí    global _session_start, _action_count
    26‚Üí    _session_start = time.monotonic()
    27‚Üí    _action_count = 0
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üídef reset_session():
    31‚Üí    """Public API: reset fatigue/distraction tracking for a new command invocation."""
    32‚Üí    _reset_session()
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üídef _lognormal_delay(min_val: float, max_val: float) -> float:
    36‚Üí    """Generate a log-normal distributed delay between min and max.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí    Log-normal produces a right-skewed distribution: most delays are
    39‚Üí    near the low end, with occasional longer pauses ‚Äî matching human behavior.
    40‚Üí    """
    41‚Üí    # Map min/max to log-normal mu/sigma
    42‚Üí    mid = (min_val + max_val) / 2
    43‚Üí    mu = math.log(mid)
    44‚Üí    sigma = 0.4  # Controls spread; 0.4 gives moderate variance
    45‚Üí    delay = random.lognormvariate(mu, sigma)
    46‚Üí    return max(min_val, min(delay, max_val * 1.5))
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üídef _fatigue_multiplier() -> float:
    50‚Üí    """Calculate fatigue multiplier based on session duration.
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí    Humans slow down over time. After 30 minutes, delays increase by ~20%.
    53‚Üí    After 1 hour, by ~40%. Caps at 1.6x.
    54‚Üí    """
    55‚Üí    elapsed_minutes = (time.monotonic() - _session_start) / 60.0
    56‚Üí    if elapsed_minutes < 10:
    57‚Üí        return 1.0
    58‚Üí    # Gradual ramp: 1.0 at 10min ‚Üí ~1.6 at 90min
    59‚Üí    factor = 1.0 + 0.006 * min(elapsed_minutes - 10, 100)
    60‚Üí    return min(factor, 1.6)
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üídef _maybe_distraction_pause() -> float:
    64‚Üí    """Occasionally return a long "distraction" pause.
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí    About 3% chance of a 5-30 second pause (simulating checking phone,
    67‚Üí    reading something, etc.). Returns 0 most of the time.
    68‚Üí    """
    69‚Üí    global _action_count
    70‚Üí    _action_count += 1
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí    # Don't trigger on first few actions
    73‚Üí    if _action_count < 5:
    74‚Üí        return 0.0
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí    if random.random() < 0.03:
    77‚Üí        pause = random.uniform(5.0, 30.0)
    78‚Üí        logger.debug(f"Simulating distraction pause: {pause:.1f}s")
    79‚Üí        return pause
    80‚Üí    return 0.0
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üídef _time_of_day_multiplier() -> float:
    84‚Üí    """Adjust delays based on time of day.
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí    Late night (23:00-07:00): slower (1.3x) ‚Äî fewer people browse late.
    87‚Üí    Peak hours (10:00-12:00, 14:00-17:00): normal (1.0x).
    88‚Üí    Other times: slightly slower (1.1x).
    89‚Üí    """
    90‚Üí    import datetime
    91‚Üí    hour = datetime.datetime.now().hour
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí    if 23 <= hour or hour < 7:
    94‚Üí        return 1.3
    95‚Üí    elif (10 <= hour <= 12) or (14 <= hour <= 17):
    96‚Üí        return 1.0
    97‚Üí    else:
    98‚Üí        return 1.1
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Pipeline checkpoint manager for resume capability.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíSaves pipeline progress to a JSON file so that interrupted pipelines
     4‚Üícan be resumed from where they left off.
     5‚Üí"""
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üíimport json
     8‚Üíimport os
     9‚Üíimport tempfile
    10‚Üífrom datetime import datetime
    11‚Üífrom pathlib import Path
    12‚Üífrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí
    17‚ÜíCHECKPOINT_DIR = Path("data/.checkpoints")
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üíclass CheckpointManager:
    21‚Üí    """Manages pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume support."""
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí    def __init__(self, checkpoint_id: Optional[str] = None):
    24‚Üí        CHECKPOINT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí        if checkpoint_id:
    27‚Üí            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"{checkpoint_id}.json"
    28‚Üí        else:
    29‚Üí            # Generate ID from timestamp
    30‚Üí            ts = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    31‚Üí            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"pipeline_{ts}.json"
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí        self._data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí    def save(
    36‚Üí        self,
    37‚Üí        keywords: tuple,
    38‚Üí        template: str,
    39‚Üí        variables: dict,
    40‚Üí        company: tuple,
    41‚Üí        location: Optional[str],
    42‚Üí        max_pages: int,
    43‚Üí        batch_pages: int,
    44‚Üí        skip_messaged: bool,
    45‚Üí        interactive: bool,
    46‚Üí        completed_pages: int,
    47‚Üí        sent_ids: List[str],
    48‚Üí        seen_ids: List[str],
    49‚Üí        total_results: Optional[dict] = None,
    50‚Üí        mode: str = "search_center",
    51‚Üí    ) -> None:
    52‚Üí        """Save current pipeline state to checkpoint file."""
    53‚Üí        self._data = {
    54‚Üí            "created_at": self._data.get("created_at", datetime.utcnow().isoformat()),
    55‚Üí            "updated_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    56‚Üí            "params": {
    57‚Üí                "keywords": list(keywords),
    58‚Üí                "template": template,
    59‚Üí                "variables": variables,
    60‚Üí                "company": list(company) if company else [],
    61‚Üí                "location": location,
    62‚Üí                "max_pages": max_pages,
    63‚Üí                "batch_pages": batch_pages,
    64‚Üí                "skip_messaged": skip_messaged,
    65‚Üí                "interactive": interactive,
    66‚Üí                "mode": mode,
    67‚Üí            },
    68‚Üí            "progress": {
    69‚Üí                "completed_pages": completed_pages,
    70‚Üí                "sent_ids": sent_ids,
    71‚Üí                "seen_ids": seen_ids,
    72‚Üí                "total_results": total_results or {},
    73‚Üí            },
    74‚Üí        }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí        try:
    77‚Üí            tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(
    78‚Üí                dir=self.checkpoint_path.parent, suffix=".tmp"
    79‚Üí            )
    80‚Üí            try:
    81‚Üí                with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    82‚Üí                    json.dump(self._data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    83‚Üí                os.replace(tmp_path, self.checkpoint_path)
    84‚Üí            except BaseException:
    85‚Üí                try:
    86‚Üí                    os.unlink(tmp_path)
    87‚Üí                except OSError:
    88‚Üí                    pass
    89‚Üí                raise
    90‚Üí            logger.debug(f"Checkpoint saved: {self.checkpoint_path}")
    91‚Üí        except Exception as e:
    92‚Üí            logger.error(f"Failed to save checkpoint: {e}")
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí    @classmethod
    95‚Üí    def load_latest(cls) -> Optional["CheckpointManager"]:
    96‚Üí        """Load the most recent checkpoint file.
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí        Returns:
    99‚Üí            CheckpointManager with loaded data, or None if no checkpoint exists.
   100‚Üí        """
   101‚Üí        CHECKPOINT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   102‚Üí        files = sorted(CHECKPOINT_DIR.glob("pipeline_*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí        if not files:
   105‚Üí            return None
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí        mgr = cls.__new__(cls)
   108‚Üí        mgr.checkpoint_path = files[0]
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí        try:
   111‚Üí            with open(files[0], "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   112‚Üí                mgr._data = json.load(f)
   113‚Üí            logger.info(f"Loaded checkpoint: {files[0]}")
   114‚Üí            return mgr
   115‚Üí        except Exception as e:
   116‚Üí            logger.error(f"Failed to load checkpoint: {e}")
   117‚Üí            return None
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí    @property
   120‚Üí    def params(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   121‚Üí        return self._data.get("params", {})
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí    @property
   124‚Üí    def progress(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   125‚Üí        return self._data.get("progress", {})
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí    @property
   128‚Üí    def completed_pages(self) -> int:
   129‚Üí        return self.progress.get("completed_pages", 0)
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí    @property
   132‚Üí    def sent_ids(self) -> List[str]:
   133‚Üí        return self.progress.get("sent_ids", [])
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí    @property
   136‚Üí    def seen_ids(self) -> Set[str]:
   137‚Üí        return set(self.progress.get("seen_ids", []))
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí    @property
   140‚Üí    def total_results(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
   141‚Üí        return self.progress.get("total_results", {})
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí    @property
   144‚Üí    def summary(self) -> str:
   145‚Üí        """Human-readable summary of checkpoint state."""
   146‚Üí        p = self.params
   147‚Üí        pr = self.progress
   148‚Üí        created = self._data.get("created_at", "unknown")
   149‚Üí        updated = self._data.get("updated_at", "unknown")
   150‚Üí        mode = p.get("mode", "search_center")
   151‚Üí        return (
   152‚Üí            f"Keywords: {p.get('keywords', [])}\n"
   153‚Üí            f"Template: {p.get('template', '')}\n"
   154‚Üí            f"Mode: {mode}\n"
   155‚Üí            f"Pages completed: {pr.get('completed_pages', 0)} / {p.get('max_pages', '?')}\n"
   156‚Üí            f"Candidates seen: {len(pr.get('seen_ids', []))}\n"
   157‚Üí            f"Messages sent: {len(pr.get('sent_ids', []))}\n"
   158‚Üí            f"Created: {created}\n"
   159‚Üí            f"Last updated: {updated}"
   160‚Üí        )
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üí    def delete(self) -> None:
   163‚Üí        """Remove checkpoint file after successful completion."""
   164‚Üí        try:
   165‚Üí            if self.checkpoint_path.exists():
   166‚Üí                self.checkpoint_path.unlink()
   167‚Üí                logger.debug(f"Checkpoint deleted: {self.checkpoint_path}")
   168‚Üí        except Exception as e:
   169‚Üí            logger.error(f"Failed to delete checkpoint: {e}")

1‚Üí"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üífrom contextlib import contextmanager
     4‚Üífrom datetime import datetime
     5‚Üífrom pathlib import Path
     6‚Üífrom typing import Optional
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üífrom sqlalchemy import (
     9‚Üí    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    10‚Üí    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint
    11‚Üí)
    12‚Üífrom sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
    13‚Üífrom sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
    14‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üífrom src.utils.config import get_config
    17‚Üí
    18‚ÜíBase = declarative_base()
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üíclass Candidate(Base):
    22‚Üí    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí    __tablename__ = 'candidates'
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    27‚Üí    maimai_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    28‚Üí    name = Column(String(100))
    29‚Üí    title = Column(String(200))
    30‚Üí    company = Column(String(200))
    31‚Üí    location = Column(String(100))
    32‚Üí    profile_url = Column(Text)
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí    # JSON fields for structured data
    35‚Üí    basic_info = Column(JSON)
    36‚Üí    education = Column(JSON)
    37‚Üí    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    38‚Üí    skills = Column(JSON)
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí    # Metadata
    41‚Üí    first_seen = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    42‚Üí    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow)
    43‚Üí    source_search = Column(Text)  # Search keywords that found this candidate
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí    # Relationships
    46‚Üí    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí    def __repr__(self):
    49‚Üí        return f"<Candidate(maimai_id='{self.maimai_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üíclass Message(Base):
    53‚Üí    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí    __tablename__ = 'messages'
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    58‚Üí    candidate_maimai_id = Column(String(50), ForeignKey('candidates.maimai_id'), index=True)
    59‚Üí    template_name = Column(String(100))
    60‚Üí    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    61‚Üí    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    62‚Üí    retry_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    63‚Üí    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    64‚Üí    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí    # Relationships
    67‚Üí    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí    def __repr__(self):
    70‚Üí        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_maimai_id}', status='{self.status}')>"
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üíclass SearchHistory(Base):
    74‚Üí    """Search history model - tracks all searches performed."""
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí    __tablename__ = 'search_history'
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    79‚Üí    criteria = Column(JSON)  # Search parameters as JSON
    80‚Üí    results_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    81‚Üí    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, index=True)
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí    def __repr__(self):
    84‚Üí        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üíclass ActionLog(Base):
    88‚Üí    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    93‚Üí    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)  # 'search', 'view_profile', 'send_message'
    94‚Üí    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)
    95‚Üí    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí    # Composite index for efficient rate limiting queries
    98‚Üí    __table_args__ = (
    99‚Üí        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
   100‚Üí    )
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí    def __repr__(self):
   103‚Üí        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üíclass DatabaseManager:
   107‚Üí    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
   110‚Üí        """Initialize database manager."""
   111‚Üí        if db_path is None:
   112‚Üí            config = get_config()
   113‚Üí            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí        # Ensure data directory exists
   116‚Üí        db_file = Path(db_path)
   117‚Üí        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí        # Create engine and session
   120‚Üí        self.db_path = db_path
   121‚Üí        self.engine = create_engine(f'sqlite:///{db_path}', echo=False)
   122‚Üí        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí        # Auto-create tables and run migrations
   125‚Üí        self.init_db()
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí    def init_db(self):
   130‚Üí        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
   131‚Üí        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
   132‚Üí        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
   135‚Üí        run_migrations(self.engine)
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üí    @contextmanager
   138‚Üí    def session_scope(self):
   139‚Üí        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
   140‚Üí        session = self.Session()
   141‚Üí        try:
   142‚Üí            yield session
   143‚Üí            session.commit()
   144‚Üí        except Exception:
   145‚Üí            session.rollback()
   146‚Üí            raise
   147‚Üí        finally:
   148‚Üí            session.close()
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí    def drop_all(self):
   151‚Üí        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
   152‚Üí        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
   153‚Üí        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí# Global database manager instance
   157‚Üí_db_manager = None
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí
   160‚Üídef get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
   161‚Üí    """Get global database manager instance."""
   162‚Üí    global _db_manager
   163‚Üí    if _db_manager is None:
   164‚Üí        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
   165‚Üí    return _db_manager
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üídef init_database():
   169‚Üí    """Initialize database with all tables."""
   170‚Üí    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   171‚Üí    db_manager.init_db()
   172‚Üí

568‚Üídef render_template(template_content: str, variables: Dict[str, str]) -> str:
   569‚Üí    """
   570‚Üí    Render template with variables.
   571‚Üí
   572‚Üí    Variable values are HTML-entity-escaped to prevent injection if the
   573‚Üí    platform renders messages as HTML.
   574‚Üí
   575‚Üí    Args:
   576‚Üí        template_content: Template string with {variable} placeholders
   577‚Üí        variables: Variable values to substitute
   578‚Üí
   579‚Üí    Returns:
   580‚Üí        Rendered message content
   581‚Üí    """
   582‚Üí    import html
   583‚Üí
   584‚Üí    content = template_content
   585‚Üí    for key, value in variables.items():
   586‚Üí        safe_value = html.escape(str(value))
   587‚Üí        content = content.replace(f"{{{key}}}", safe_value)
   588‚Üí    return content
   589‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Pipeline checkpoint manager for resume capability.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíSaves pipeline progress to a JSON file so that interrupted pipelines
     4‚Üícan be resumed from where they left off.
     5‚Üí"""
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üíimport json
     8‚Üíimport os
     9‚Üíimport tempfile
    10‚Üífrom datetime import datetime
    11‚Üífrom pathlib import Path
    12‚Üífrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí
    17‚ÜíCHECKPOINT_DIR = Path("data/.checkpoints")
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üíclass CheckpointManager:
    21‚Üí    """Manages pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume support."""
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí    def __init__(self, checkpoint_id: Optional[str] = None):
    24‚Üí        CHECKPOINT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí        if checkpoint_id:
    27‚Üí            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"{checkpoint_id}.json"
    28‚Üí        else:
    29‚Üí            # Generate ID from timestamp
    30‚Üí            ts = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    31‚Üí            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"pipeline_{ts}.json"
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí        self._data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí    def save(
    36‚Üí        self,
    37‚Üí        keywords: tuple,
    38‚Üí        template: str,
    39‚Üí        variables: dict,
    40‚Üí        company: tuple,
    41‚Üí        location: Optional[str],
    42‚Üí        max_pages: int,
    43‚Üí        batch_pages: int,
    44‚Üí        skip_messaged: bool,
    45‚Üí        interactive: bool,
    46‚Üí        completed_pages: int,
    47‚Üí        sent_ids: List[str],
    48‚Üí        seen_ids: List[str],
    49‚Üí        total_results: Optional[dict] = None,
    50‚Üí        mode: str = "search_center",
    51‚Üí    ) -> None:
    52‚Üí        """Save current pipeline state to checkpoint file."""
    53‚Üí        self._data = {
    54‚Üí            "created_at": self._data.get("created_at", datetime.utcnow().isoformat()),
    55‚Üí            "updated_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    56‚Üí            "params": {
    57‚Üí                "keywords": list(keywords),
    58‚Üí                "template": template,
    59‚Üí                "variables": variables,
    60‚Üí                "company": list(company) if company else [],
    61‚Üí                "location": location,
    62‚Üí                "max_pages": max_pages,
    63‚Üí                "batch_pages": batch_pages,
    64‚Üí                "skip_messaged": skip_messaged,
    65‚Üí                "interactive": interactive,
    66‚Üí                "mode": mode,
    67‚Üí            },
    68‚Üí            "progress": {
    69‚Üí                "completed_pages": completed_pages,
    70‚Üí                "sent_ids": sent_ids,
    71‚Üí                "seen_ids": seen_ids,
    72‚Üí                "total_results": total_results or {},
    73‚Üí            },
    74‚Üí        }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí        try:
    77‚Üí            tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(
    78‚Üí                dir=self.checkpoint_path.parent, suffix=".tmp"
    79‚Üí            )
    80‚Üí            try:
    81‚Üí                with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    82‚Üí                    json.dump(self._data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    83‚Üí                os.replace(tmp_path, self.checkpoint_path)
    84‚Üí            except BaseException:
    85‚Üí                try:
    86‚Üí                    os.unlink(tmp_path)
    87‚Üí                except OSError:
    88‚Üí                    pass
    89‚Üí                raise
    90‚Üí            logger.debug(f"Checkpoint saved: {self.checkpoint_path}")
    91‚Üí        except Exception as e:
    92‚Üí            logger.error(f"Failed to save checkpoint: {e}")
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí    @classmethod
    95‚Üí    def load_latest(cls) -> Optional["CheckpointManager"]:
    96‚Üí        """Load the most recent checkpoint file.
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí        Returns:
    99‚Üí            CheckpointManager with loaded data, or None if no checkpoint exists.
   100‚Üí        """
